title: adesmia riograndensis
intro: Adesmia riograndensis, also known as the Rio Grande shrub or Queñua de la sierra, is a plant species native to South America, specifically in the Andean region of Argentina, Chile, and Uruguay. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Adesmia riograndensis are attributed to its ability to reduce pain, fever, and inflammation, as well as its potential to protect against infections and oxidative stress. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or infusion, and in some regions, it is also used to make a medicinal syrup or tincture.
